$comment: Canonical entity dictionary referenced by Article/TechArticle/Transcript JSON-LD across the site. Frontmatter `about:` (single primary) and `mentions:` (array of secondaries) reference the slugs below. Entries are not displayed verbatim to readers — they're the @id resolver for structured-data emission. Add an entry once and every Article-family page that references it via slug reinforces the same canonical entity in Google's Knowledge Graph + AI Overview ingestion.
$schema_note: Slug naming convention: use the project's canonical brand spelling. If the brand has internal capitalization (`wasmCloud`, `wash`), preserve it. Acronyms in their canonical form (WASI, MCP, CNCF, NATS, JCO). Multi-word entities without a single-token canonical form use CamelCase (ComponentModel, BytecodeAlliance). The slug is just the resolver key — the human-readable form lives in each entry's `name` field and is what consumers see in JSON-LD.

WebAssembly.name: WebAssembly
WebAssembly.description: A portable binary instruction format for a stack-based virtual machine, providing memory-safe sandboxed execution.

WASI.name: WASI
WASI.description: Standardized APIs for WebAssembly modules to interact with the host operating system, including filesystem, network, clocks, and process control.

ComponentModel.name: Component Model
ComponentModel.description: The WebAssembly specification for composing isolated components through typed interfaces, enabling polyglot composition across languages.

WASIPreview3.name: WASI Preview 3
WASIPreview3.description: The next major revision of the WebAssembly System Interface, adding native async support, streams, and futures to the Component Model.

Wasmtime.name: Wasmtime
Wasmtime.description: A standalone, fast, and secure runtime for WebAssembly. wasmCloud embeds Wasmtime as its WebAssembly engine.

JCO.name: JCO
JCO.description: JavaScript toolchain for WebAssembly Component Model — transpiles components to ES modules and includes WASI Preview 3 reference implementation.

ComponentizeJS.name: ComponentizeJS
ComponentizeJS.description: Toolchain for authoring WebAssembly components from JavaScript source.

StarlingMonkey.name: StarlingMonkey
StarlingMonkey.description: SpiderMonkey-based JavaScript engine targeting WebAssembly Component Model, used as the runtime for ComponentizeJS-authored components.

wasmCloud.name: wasmCloud
wasmCloud.description: CNCF Incubating runtime that schedules WebAssembly components across cloud, Kubernetes, datacenter, and edge environments.

wash.name: wash CLI
wash.description: Command-line shell for building, running, and managing wasmCloud workloads.

wadm.name: wadm
wadm.description: Declarative deployment manager for wasmCloud workloads in v1; replaced by the Kubernetes runtime operator in v2.

runtimeOperator.name: wasmCloud Runtime Operator
runtimeOperator.description: Kubernetes operator that schedules wasmCloud components through standard Kubernetes APIs (pods, deployments, services).

capabilityProvider.name: capability provider
capabilityProvider.description: In wasmCloud v1, a NATS-connected external process providing a host capability (HTTP, messaging, KV); largely replaced by host plugins and service components in v2.

BytecodeAlliance.name: Bytecode Alliance
BytecodeAlliance.description: Nonprofit organization stewarding WebAssembly specifications (Wasm core, WASI, Component Model) and key implementations.

CNCF.name: CNCF
CNCF.description: The open-source foundation hosting wasmCloud, Kubernetes, Prometheus, and other cloud-native projects.

Cosmonic.name: Cosmonic
Cosmonic.description: Commercial company whose engineers maintain and contribute substantially to wasmCloud.

Spin.name: Spin
Spin.description: Single-host WebAssembly runtime from Fermyon focused on developer ergonomics for HTTP-style applications.

Wasmer.name: Wasmer
Wasmer.description: Universal WebAssembly runtime supporting multiple Wasm engines and language-specific embeddings.

OCIRegistry.name: OCI Registry
OCIRegistry.description: Open Container Initiative-compliant registry. wasmCloud distributes WebAssembly components and providers via OCI artifacts.

Kubernetes.name: Kubernetes
Kubernetes.description: Open-source container orchestration platform. wasmCloud v2 schedules WebAssembly workloads through native Kubernetes APIs.

NATS.name: NATS
NATS.description: Open-source distributed messaging system. wasmCloud uses NATS as the lattice transport for distributed workloads.

Docker.name: Docker
Docker.description: Container platform; wasmCloud reuses OCI / Docker registry infrastructure for WebAssembly artifact distribution.

OpenTelemetry.name: OpenTelemetry
OpenTelemetry.description: Open-source observability framework for traces, metrics, and logs. wasmCloud emits native OpenTelemetry signals.

Prometheus.name: Prometheus
Prometheus.description: Open-source monitoring and alerting toolkit; primary metrics backend for wasmCloud observability.

MCP.name: Model Context Protocol
MCP.description: Anthropic's open protocol for connecting AI models to external tools and data through a standardized server interface.

AISandbox.name: AI sandbox
AISandbox.description: Isolated execution environment for AI-generated, LLM-authored, or untrusted-by-construction code. WebAssembly's deny-by-default capability model is a natural fit.

AIOps.name: AIOps
AIOps.description: Application of artificial intelligence to IT operations — incident response, capacity planning, anomaly detection, runbook automation.

agenticAI.name: agentic AI
agenticAI.description: AI systems that autonomously plan, decide, and execute multi-step actions in response to high-level goals.

AIInfrastructure.name: AI infrastructure
AIInfrastructure.description: Compute, storage, and execution platforms underlying production AI workloads — model serving, inference, fine-tuning, agentic runtimes.

vibeCoding.name: vibe coding
vibeCoding.description: Using AI assistants to generate code through natural-language prompts with minimal manual review.

capabilityBasedSecurity.name: capability-based security
capabilityBasedSecurity.description: Security model where access is granted via unforgeable tokens rather than identity checks. Contrasts with ambient authority.

denyByDefault.name: deny-by-default
denyByDefault.description: Authorization posture where code starts with zero permissions and must be explicitly granted each capability it needs.

ambientAuthority.name: ambient authority
ambientAuthority.description: Anti-pattern where code inherits the permissions of its parent process by default. The root cause of many container and VM security failures.

blastRadius.name: blast radius
blastRadius.description: The scope of damage a vulnerability or misconfiguration can cause. WebAssembly's component boundary bounds the blast radius to the component's granted capabilities.

Rust.name: Rust
Rust.description: Systems programming language emphasizing memory safety without garbage collection. Strong WebAssembly tooling ecosystem.

Go.name: Go
Go.description: Open-source programming language with WebAssembly support via TinyGo and the standard Go toolchain.

Python.name: Python
Python.description: High-level dynamic programming language with WebAssembly component support via componentize-py.

TypeScript.name: TypeScript
TypeScript.description: Typed superset of JavaScript with WebAssembly component support via ComponentizeJS.

JavaScript.name: JavaScript
JavaScript.description: Dynamic scripting language; WebAssembly component support via ComponentizeJS / StarlingMonkey.

CSharp.name: C#
CSharp.description: Microsoft's .NET-family language with WebAssembly component support.

Java.name: Java
Java.description: JVM language with emerging WebAssembly component support via runtimes like Endive.

Cranelift.name: Cranelift
Cranelift.description: Bytecode Alliance code generator that compiles WebAssembly to native code for Wasmtime and other runtimes.

Winch.name: Winch
Winch.description: Wasmtime's baseline (single-pass) compiler — trades peak throughput for faster module compilation, used for fast cold starts and short-lived workloads.

Pulley.name: Pulley
Pulley.description: Wasmtime's portable WebAssembly interpreter — runs ~10× slower than native but enables WebAssembly on microcontrollers and constrained environments where JIT/AOT is unavailable.

witBindgen.name: wit-bindgen
witBindgen.description: Bytecode Alliance binding generator that turns WIT (WebAssembly Interface Type) definitions into guest-side language bindings for Rust, Go, C, C++, Python, JavaScript, and more.

wasmTools.name: wasm-tools
wasmTools.description: Bytecode Alliance CLI toolbox for parsing, validating, printing, transforming, and componentizing WebAssembly modules.

cargoComponent.name: cargo-component
cargoComponent.description: Cargo subcommand for building WebAssembly components from Rust source with WIT-driven bindings.

wac.name: WAC
wac.description: Bytecode Alliance language and CLI for composing WebAssembly components — declaratively wires multiple components together by their WIT interfaces.

wkg.name: wkg
wkg.description: Bytecode Alliance package manager CLI for distributing WebAssembly components and WIT interfaces via OCI registries.

Javy.name: Javy
Javy.description: Bytecode Alliance toolchain that compiles JavaScript to WebAssembly modules via the QuickJS engine — predates and complements ComponentizeJS.

Chicory.name: Chicory
Chicory.description: Pure-Java WebAssembly interpreter with no native dependencies; the upstream of the Endive runtime.

Endive.name: Endive
Endive.description: Bytecode Alliance Java WebAssembly runtime — a Chicory fork that adds a Cranelift compiler backend and is designed for zero-native-dependency JVM integration.

QuickJS.name: QuickJS
QuickJS.description: Small, embeddable JavaScript engine by Fabrice Bellard, with a blessed QuickJS-ng fork. Underpins Javy and several ComponentizeJS variants targeting WebAssembly.

GraalVM.name: GraalVM
GraalVM.description: Oracle's polyglot VM. The Web Image AOT compiler emits WebAssembly from JVM bytecode — the JVM-to-Wasm direction complementing Endive's Wasm-in-JVM direction.

Vertx.name: Eclipse Vert.x
Vertx.description: Reactive serverless toolkit for the JVM. Hosts mixed Java + WebAssembly handlers behind a single event-loop HTTP server in Endive demos.

MCPSecurity.name: MCP security
MCPSecurity.description: Threat model and isolation strategies for Model Context Protocol servers — sandboxing tool invocations, scoping credentials, and preventing prompt-injection-driven escalation. WebAssembly capability-based isolation is a natural fit.

promptInjection.name: prompt injection
promptInjection.description: Attack class where adversarial input manipulates an LLM's instructions, often via tool outputs, retrieved documents, or chained agents. Sandbox-per-call isolation is a leading mitigation.

agentSandboxing.name: agent sandboxing
agentSandboxing.description: Containment strategy for autonomous AI agents — isolating tool invocations, file access, and network calls per-agent or per-task. WebAssembly's deny-by-default capability model gives per-invocation isolation with sub-millisecond startup.

AIInference.name: AI inference
AIInference.description: Runtime execution of trained AI/ML models for prediction or generation. WebAssembly enables sandboxed, portable inference at the edge.

AISafety.name: AI safety
AISafety.description: Engineering and policy practice of containing AI behavior — covers sandboxing, capability gating, prompt-injection defenses, and runtime guardrails for both deterministic ML and agentic LLM workloads.

SQLx.name: SQLx
SQLx.description: Pure-Rust async SQL toolkit supporting Postgres, MySQL, and SQLite. Runs inside WebAssembly Preview 2 components with the Tokio current-thread runtime.

Tokio.name: Tokio
Tokio.description: Asynchronous runtime for Rust. The current-thread variant + tokio_unstable backend enables async Rust components on WebAssembly Preview 2.

PgBouncer.name: PgBouncer
PgBouncer.description: Lightweight Postgres connection pooler used at the edge of database deployments. Discussed as the canonical example for what a WebAssembly host component plugin could and could not absorb.

iaiCallgrind.name: iai-callgrind
iaiCallgrind.description: Rust benchmarking harness built on Valgrind's Callgrind — measures CPU instruction counts for low-variance, replicable performance regression testing.

Criterion.name: Criterion
Criterion.description: Statistics-driven Rust microbenchmark harness measuring wall-clock time with confidence intervals.

xtask.name: cargo xtask
xtask.description: Convention in Rust workspaces for build-and-tool tasks expressed as a regular cargo subcommand — decouples ancillary scripts from the main cargo build cycle.

zizmor.name: zizmor
zizmor.description: Static analyzer for GitHub Actions workflows that flags supply-chain and CI-security misconfigurations. Used in the wasmCloud CI hardening pass.

CodeQL.name: CodeQL
CodeQL.description: GitHub's semantic code analysis engine for finding vulnerabilities across multi-language codebases.

OpenSSFScorecard.name: OpenSSF Scorecard
OpenSSFScorecard.description: Open-source security health checker that scores projects against ~20 supply-chain best practices.

SPIFFE.name: SPIFFE
alternateName: Secure Production Identity Framework For Everyone
SPIFFE.description: CNCF identity framework that issues short-lived, cryptographically attested workload identities (X.509-SVIDs / JWT-SVIDs) — the reference identity layer for zero-trust workload-to-workload auth.

TinyGo.name: TinyGo
TinyGo.description: Alternative Go compiler targeting WebAssembly and embedded systems with a smaller standard library.
